title: Catalyst Center + Meraki Branch Network Health
dataSources: `index=catalyst sourcetype=cisco:dnac:networkhealth`. from the `networkhealth` modular input in the Cisco Catalyst Add-on (Splunkbase 7538) polling `GET /dna/intent/api/v1/network-health`. Default poll interval: 900s (15m). Volume: ~1 event per poll (aggregate network score). RBAC: NETWORK-ADMIN-ROLE. Key fields: `healthScore` (integer 0-100), `goodCount` (integer), `badCount` (integer), `totalCount` (integer). Additionally correlates with `index=meraki sourcetype=meraki:api` (Meraki network health via the Cisco Meraki TA, Splunkbase). Requires both Catalyst Center and Meraki data collection.
app: `Cisco Catalyst Add-on for Splunk` (Splunkbase 7538) + `Cisco Meraki Add-on for Splunk` (Splunkbase 5580)
spl: index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:networkhealth" | stats latest(healthScore) as campus_health by _time | appendcols [search index=cisco_network sourcetype="meraki:api" | stats avg(health_score) as branch_health count(eval(status="offline")) as offline_branches by _time] | eval campus_vs_branch=campus_health-branch_health | where abs(campus_vs_branch) > 15 | table _time campus_health branch_health offline_branches campus_vs_branch
description: Compares Catalyst Center campus network health with Meraki branch network health to identify divergence between campus and branch office performance.
value: Many organizations use Catalyst Center for campus and Meraki for branches. Comparing both reveals whether network problems are campus-specific, branch-specific, or universal.
implementation:
Deploy **Cisco Catalyst Add-on (7538)** for `cisco:dnac:networkhealth` on `index=catalyst` and **Cisco Meraki Add-on (5580)** for organization API data as `sourcetype=meraki:api` (commonly `index=cisco_network` or your org standard).

1. **Meraki API:** In the Meraki TA, add an org API key and enable the inputs that produce device/network health and status (health or summary endpoints per TA version).
2. **Field names:** The SPL uses `health_score` and `status` on Meraki events — if your field names differ (`uplinkStatus`, `device status`), map them with `eval` before `stats`.
3. **Time sync:** `appendcols` is row-aligned; for production, bin `_time` on both sides to the same span (e.g. 5m) or use a **join** on discrete `_time` buckets.
4. **Thresholds:** Adjust `15` to match the noise floor for your environment.
visualization: Dual-axis line: campus_health vs branch_health; table when `abs(campus_vs_branch) > 15`; optional single value for `offline_branches` with a threshold.
knownFalsePositives:
**Meraki cloud connectivity issue causing stale Meraki health data while campus data is current.** If the Meraki dashboard API is slow or unavailable, the Meraki health data in Splunk may be stale while Catalyst Center data is fresh. Distinguish by comparing the latest `_time` from both sourcetypes. Suppress by alerting when the Meraki data staleness exceeds 2x the expected poll interval.

**Different health score scales between Catalyst Center and Meraki.** Catalyst Center uses 0-100 while Meraki may use a different scale or calculation method. A direct numeric comparison may not be meaningful. Distinguish by checking the health score normalization — if Meraki scores cluster around 80-100 while campus scores cluster around 60-80, the scales may not be comparable. Suppress by normalizing both scores to percentile rank within their own distribution before comparing.

**Branch site maintenance causing Meraki health drop for one location while campus is stable.** A single branch office undergoing renovation or equipment replacement will lower the Meraki average. Distinguish by checking whether the Meraki health drop is localized to one network or site. Do not suppress — enrich the alert with the specific branch context.

**Meraki API rate limiting causing incomplete health data.** The Meraki Dashboard API has rate limits that may prevent the TA from collecting health data for all organizations/networks in a single poll. Distinguish by checking `index=_internal` for Meraki TA errors related to HTTP 429 rate limiting. Suppress by increasing the Meraki poll interval or splitting organizations across multiple inputs.

references.title: Catalyst Center Network Health API — Cisco DevNet

detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Cisco Catalyst Add-on for Splunk (Splunkbase 7538) with `devicehealth` inputs enabled to `index=catalyst`.
• Cisco Meraki Add-on for Splunk (Splunkbase 3018 or later) with device and network health data in `index=meraki` (or equivalent), sourcetype `cisco:meraki:*`.
• Both Catalyst Center and Meraki Dashboard API credentials configured in their respective TAs.
• Network: heavy forwarder must reach both Catalyst Center API and Meraki Dashboard API (api.meraki.com).

Step 1 — Configure data collection
This UC correlates campus network health (Catalyst Center) with branch network health (Meraki):
• **Catalyst Center side:** `sourcetype=cisco:dnac:devicehealth` — polled from `GET /dna/intent/api/v1/device-health` at 900s intervals.
• **Meraki side:** `sourcetype=cisco:meraki:device:status` or `cisco:meraki:network:health` — polled from the Meraki Dashboard API at the TA's configured interval.

Key fields for correlation:
• Catalyst Center: `deviceName`, `overallHealth`, `reachabilityHealth`, `siteId`.
• Meraki: `networkId`, `name`, `status`, `publicIp`, `lanIp`.
• Correlation: by site name or a `catalyst_meraki_site_map` lookup mapping Catalyst Center `siteId` to Meraki `networkId`.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert

```spl
index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:devicehealth"
| stats latest(overallHealth) as cc_health latest(reachabilityHealth) as cc_reach dc(deviceName) as cc_devices by siteId
| lookup catalyst_meraki_site_map siteId OUTPUT networkId, site_name
| join networkId type=left [search index=meraki sourcetype="cisco:meraki:*" | stats latest(status) as meraki_status dc(serial) as meraki_devices by networkId]
| eval branch_status=case(cc_reach="Unreachable" AND meraki_status!="online","Branch Offline", cc_health<50 AND meraki_status!="online","Both Degraded", cc_health<50,"Campus Degraded", meraki_status!="online","Meraki Branch Down", 1==1,"Healthy")
| table site_name, cc_health, cc_devices, meraki_status, meraki_devices, branch_status
| sort branch_status
```

Understanding this SPL:
• **`lookup catalyst_meraki_site_map`**: Translates between Catalyst Center site UUIDs and Meraki network IDs. This lookup must be manually created and maintained as there is no automatic mapping between the two systems.
• **`join networkId type=left`**: Left join ensures all Catalyst Center sites appear even if no Meraki data exists (campus-only sites).
• **`branch_status=case(...)`**: Combined assessment prioritizing complete branch outages.

Step 3 — Validate
• **Cross-reference:** Select 3-5 branch sites that have both Catalyst Center-managed switches and Meraki equipment. Compare the health status in both Splunk and the respective vendor UIs.
• **Lookup accuracy:** Verify the `catalyst_meraki_site_map` lookup covers all sites with both Catalyst Center and Meraki presence.

Step 4 — Operationalize
• **Dashboard:** Branch health overview with color-coded status tiles per site. Drill down to individual device health for each branch.
• **Alert:** Trigger on "Branch Offline" — indicates a complete branch outage affecting both campus and SD-WAN/Meraki connectivity. Route to NOC for immediate attention.

Step 5 — Troubleshoot
• **Missing Meraki data:** Verify the Meraki TA API key has not expired, and that the organization ID is correct. Check `index=meraki earliest=-1h | stats count` for data flow.
• **Lookup mapping incomplete:** New sites added in either Catalyst Center or Meraki Dashboard need to be added to the lookup. Consider automating lookup generation from both APIs.
• **Time skew:** Meraki API timestamps are UTC; ensure Catalyst Center timestamps are also in UTC or that the SPL normalizes time zones before correlation.
• **No networkhealth events arriving:** verify the `networkhealth` input is enabled and check `index=_internal` for API error or endpoint timeout messages from the TA.
• **NULL or zero values in health scores:** confirm Assurance is licensed and enabled; missing health values indicate Assurance analytics is not computing for the site.
• **Fewer records than expected:** the network health API returns one aggregate event per poll; if credential access is denied, check the service account role permissions.
grandmaExplanation: We compare how your main office network and branch office networks are doing to see if problems are hitting everywhere or just certain locations.
